full_prompt: Design the drivetrain frame of a student-robotics competition robot for a 2025 edition. The robot must fit within a stowed-configuration envelope of approximately 1000 mm x 1000 mm x 1500 mm (L x W x H). The combined mass of all robots per team must not exceed 50 kg (if one robot, all mass budget applies; if manual+automatic robots, the combined mass must remain under 50 kg). The primary battery is limited to 24 V nominal. Any pneumatic actuators must operate at a gauge pressure no greater than 600 kPa (0.6 MPa) and must be supplied from a reservoir certified for that pressure. The drivetrain frame must carry (a) the robot's own self-weight (40 kg assumed for a single-robot design) under a 2 g vertical shock equivalent (80 kg equivalent) with no permanent yield, (b) a 200 N horizontal tipping-recovery force applied at the top of the robot, (c) four-point lifting via hand-holds or a gantry interface where each hand-hold carries 20 kg under 1.5 g shock, representing tournament handling. Primary verification is linear static structural FEA for the frame under the three load cases. Pneumatic reservoir pressure certification and voltage rating are regulatory / component-specification checks (non-FEA). Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required at the mechanical design gate. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the drivetrain frame, a mass budget, and a report listing total robot mass in kg, max von Mises stress per load case in MPa, and max frame deflection per load case in mm.
short_prompt:
Design the drivetrain frame of a student robotics competition robot for a 2025 edition. Stowed envelope approximately 1000 x 1000 x 1500 mm (L x W x H). Combined team robot mass <= 50 kg. Battery 24 V nominal; pneumatic actuators at <= 600 kPa gauge from a certified reservoir (component-certification check).

Load cases on the frame (quasi-static equivalents).
- LC1 self-weight 2 g shock: 40 kg single-robot x 2 g vertical (~785 N).
- LC2 tipping-recovery: 200 N horizontal at the top of the robot.
- LC3 four-point lifting (tournament handling): 20 kg at each handhold x 1.5 g (~294 N per handhold).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: 24 V battery and 600 kPa pneumatic cert are catalog / datasheet lookups, not simulation.

prompt.title: Student robotics drivetrain frame
prompt.brief: Design a drivetrain frame fitting 1 m x 1 m x 1.5 m, total robot mass ≤ 50 kg, 24 V battery, pneumatic ≤ 600 kPa.

load_cases.name: lifting_handhold
load_cases.description: 20 kg x 1.5 g per handhold, 4 points

boundary_conditions.wheels: fixed at contact for LC1, LC2
